The search confirmed the existence of such an agreement, with the DRC set to temporarily host third-country nationals deported from the US starting in April 2026. To get precise details on the financing aspect mentioned by the Congolese government, I browsed specific sources, including an RFI article and an official government communiqué on Facebook.

This directly aligns with option (A), as it specifies that the Congolese government will bear no financial costs. None of the other options (e.g., direct payments of 32 million dollars, shared costs, or linkages to mining agreements) were mentioned in the official statements or related coverage.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

- La mission Artemis II est une mission circumlunaire habitée prévue pour dépasser le record de distance établi par Apollo 13 (400 171 km ou 248 655 miles).
- Selon plusieurs sources récentes de la NASA et d'autres médias, la distance maximale prévue pour Artemis II est d'environ 252 760 miles (soit approximativement 406 777 km en conversion standard). Cependant, des sources spécifiques, y compris des rapports en français alignés sur la date du 6 avril 2026, citent précisément 406 840 km comme la prévision de la NASA pour cette distance maximale.
- Cette valeur (406 840 km) correspond exactement à l'option (B) et est cohérente avec les prévisions pour briser le record d'Apollo 13 par environ 6 669 km.
- Les autres options incluent le record d'Apollo 13 (C), des distances lunaires moyennes ou approximatives (A, E), ou une valeur légèrement supérieure mais non citée (D).

(A) Une pénurie naturelle due à l'épuisement des réserves mondiales.
(B) La fermeture accidentelle du détroit d'Ormuz par des navires de guerre.
(C) Une augmentation soudaine de la demande industrielle en Europe occidentale.

Based on my search and analysis of relevant sources, the first oil shock of October 1973, also known as the 1973 oil crisis, was triggered during the Yom Kippur War. Economist Carol Nakhle specifically attributes it to a deliberate political action by Arab oil-producing countries, who imposed an oil embargo and reduced production in response to Western support for Israel. This is not a natural shortage, accidental event, demand surge, or unrelated modern conflict like the Ukraine invasion.

A direct quote from Nakhle highlights this: "that earlier shock was driven by a deliberate political decision by Arab producers to impose an oil embargo and cut production, which caused prices to surge sharply within a short period."[[1]](https://www.crystolenergy.com/media_posts/what-was-the-1970s-oil-crisis) This aligns perfectly with option (D).

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS :
- [Crystol Energy] (https://www.crystolenergy.com/media_posts/what-was-the-1970s-oil-crisis)

Voici mon raisonnement étape par étape pour répondre à cette question.

La question porte sur les conseils du Dr Khadija Mormoni concernant la consommation d'eau tiède (éventuellement citronnée) immédiatement après le réveil. Pour trouver la réponse, j'ai effectué une recherche web sur le sujet, ce qui m'a conduit à un article de BBC News Afrique publié récemment (le 5 avril 2026). J'ai ensuite consulté le contenu détaillé de cet article pour extraire les informations précises.

Dans cet article, le Dr Khadija Mormoni, médecin au département de santé communautaire de l'hôpital universitaire de l'État de Kaduna à Barau Dikko, explique que boire de l'eau tiède (citronnée ou non) aide à nettoyer les intestins et l'estomac. En comparant cela aux options proposées :
- (A) Parle d'augmenter la tension artérielle, ce qui n'est pas mentionné.
- (B) Correspond exactement : nettoyer les intestins et éliminer les restes d'aliments de la veille.
- (C) Évoque la réduction du taux de sucre, non mentionné.
- (D) Parle d'hydrater le cerveau et de la mémoire, non pertinent ici.
- (E) Concerne la lumière bleue des téléphones, sans rapport.

La réponse correcte est donc (B).

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS :
- [BBC News Afrique] (https://www.bbc.com/afrique/articles/ce9yy9my3jno)

- (D) Involves recent Houthi actions (post-2010s) in the Red Sea/Suez area, not 1987 or Hormuz.

<final_answer>

model_output:
According to the query, we are on April 6, 2026, and the Human Rights Watch (HRW) report in question was published last Thursday (which calculates to April 2, 2026, assuming standard calendar progression). The report focuses on civilian deaths in Burkina Faso since Ibrahim Traoré's takeover (noted as effective from late 2022, but with data starting from 2023 as per the question's framing).

Based on web search results, the relevant HRW report is titled "“None Can Run Away”: War Crimes and Crimes Against Humanity in Burkina Faso by All Sides," published on April 2, 2026[[1]](https://www.hrw.org/report/2026/04/02/none-can-run-away/war-crimes-and-crimes-against-humanity-in-burkina-faso-by-all). It documents that the Burkina Faso military, allied militias, and Al Qaeda-linked armed groups have killed more than 1,800 civilians since 2023[[2]](https://www.hrw.org/news/2026/04/02/burkina-faso-crimes-against-humanity-by-all-sides)[[3]](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/2/burkina-faso-military-allies-committing-horrific-civilian-abuses-hrw).

Further details from browsing the report itself confirm at least 1,837 civilian deaths across 57 incidents from January 2023 to August 2025, described as potentially undercounted and "the tip of the iceberg"[[1]](https://www.hrw.org/report/2026/04/02/none-can-run-away/war-crimes-and-crimes-against-humanity-in-burkina-faso-by-all). This figure includes killings by multiple perpetrators (military, VDPs/militias, and Islamist groups like JNIM), not limited to one side. The report emphasizes war crimes and crimes against humanity by all involved parties.

Comparing to the options:
- (A) Does not match; the figure is much higher, and deaths are not mainly from political demonstrations but from military operations, massacres, and insurgent actions.
- (B) Refers to over 10,000 deaths over ten years (aligning with broader ACLED estimates since 2016[[1]](https://www.hrw.org/report/2026/04/02/none-can-run-away/war-crimes-and-crimes-against-humanity-in-burkina-faso-by-all)), but the HRW report specifies since 2023, with a lower figure for that period.
- (C) Incorrect; no exact figure of 1,200 is cited, and while there is a partial breakdown (e.g., some incidents attribute deaths to Islamists), it's not two-thirds.
- (D) Close but inaccurate; the report cites at least/more than 1,800, not exactly 1,800, and deaths are by multiple actors (including army, militias, and Islamists), not solely the regular army.
- (E) Matches directly; the report consistently states "more than 1,800 civilians" killed since 2023.

Thus, the best answer is (E).

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>
